response:
Sec. 1  
People who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their bodies react to the protein in peanuts even when they are not in contact with water  
The allergic reaction is triggered by the immune system and is not affected by the environment in which the person lives  
Living underwater does not eliminate the presence of peanuts or the risk of exposure to them  

Sec. 2  
The immune system responds to allergens regardless of the setting in which they are encountered  
Therefore the inability to live underwater is unrelated to the presence of peanuts or the nature of the allergy  
This highlights the importance of understanding that allergies are biological responses not influenced by external conditions
